title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Gen Z is increasingly struggling with loneliness and dating app Hinge is expanding its One More Hour social impact initiative. Hinge announced a $1 million fund to help social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London put on free or affordable social events for young people.
bullets: Gen Z facing rising loneliness, per description
Hinge expanding One More Hour initiative globally
Announced $1 million fund to support social groups
Target cities: New York, Los Angeles, London
Funds aimed at free or affordable events for young people
takeaways: The description contains no infrastructure, traffic, or energy metrics relevant to grid/data-center planning.
There is no stated indication that the $1M fund will drive increased digital infrastructure demand.
Based on available information, there are no immediate operational implications for grid or data-center capacity.
If capacity concerns arise, request engagement/usage metrics or campaign details from the company before adjusting resources.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this session shows how to automatically build a knowledge graph from raw documents and connect it to an AI agent so the agent understands your company data. The presenter demonstrates an end-to-end build with no graph expertise required.
bullets: AI agents often lack knowledge of a specific company's data; knowledge graphs address that gap.
The session builds a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents.
The graph is connected to an AI agent so it can understand business-specific information.
No prior graph theory or graph-query skills are required.
An end-to-end demo is shown; a prior episode compares VectorDB vs GraphDB for Gen AI agents.
takeaways: Knowledge graphs can enable AI agents to make use of enterprise documents and internal knowledge.
Automated graph construction lowers the need for specialized graph expertise when deploying agent solutions.
An end-to-end, no-expertise-required approach suggests faster paths to integrate AI agents with internal data.
Decision-makers should consider how enterprise document stores and knowledge ingestion pipelines will be used by agent-driven workflows.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Randa Barsoum, Tax Partner at PwC US, highlights tax credits and incentives for sustainability that go beyond energy incentives. She points to state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation (for example, reimagining packaging or processes), stressing that a broader view of incentives can unlock meaningful tax value from sustainability investments.
bullets: Energy incentives are common but only part of the tax picture for sustainability projects.
State and local abatements can support sustainability-related investments.
Location-based incentives may affect project siting and economics.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation efforts, including reimagining packaging or processes.
Viewing sustainability investments through a broader incentives lens can unlock meaningful tax value.
takeaways: Include state and local abatements and location-based incentives in financial models for new sites or upgrades.
Screen sustainability projects for potential R&D tax credits when they involve process or technology innovation.
Expand incentive assessments beyond energy credits to capture additional tax value from sustainability investments.
Factor jurisdictional incentives into siting and cost-benefit decisions.

items.title: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star 🌟

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Copilot helps Christa "find the star within" in a short, star-themed message. The clip invites viewers to download and try Copilot via the link in the channel's bio.
bullets: No transcript available; description-only content.
Theme: the sky/stars and finding an "inner star."
Copilot is presented as helping Christa discover personal potential.
Call-to-action: "Download & try Copilot" via link in bio.
Published by the Microsoft Copilot channel.
takeaways: User adoption: The video positions Copilot as a consumer-facing, personal-assistance tool—consider evaluating user-facing AI experiences when planning deployments.
Onboarding & access: The simple CTA (link in bio) underscores the value of frictionless trial access for driving uptake.
Messaging & internal advocacy: Human-centered stories can support internal pilot programs and help justify trials of AI capabilities.
Content & metadata: Missing transcripts limit accessibility and auditability—ensure content used in decisioning is properly documented.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this GeForce Garage video announces the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build Contest — u/JeffersonPutnam — and showcases their small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. The description lists the full build parts (Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition, MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS) and links to the PCPartPicker list and GeForce social channels.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build featured.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2 (small form factor).
Key components: GeForce RTX 5090 FE, MSI MPG B850I Edge, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000.
Power and cooling: Corsair SF1000 (SFX) and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
PCPartPicker link and GeForce social links provided in the description.
takeaways: The showcased system pairs a high-end consumer GPU (GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition) with a compact SFF chassis — note when assessing consumer-GPU use in space-constrained deployments.
Power delivery in compact builds relied on a 1000W SFX unit (Corsair SF1000) — consider SFX/space and power-source implications for dense deployments.
Cooling in the SFF build uses a 240mm AIO (Corsair Nautilus 240 RS), indicating liquid cooling solutions may be employed for thermal management in tight enclosures.
The build combines a mini-ITX platform (MSI MPG B850I Edge) with a high-performance CPU (Ryzen 7 9800X3D), showing that high CPU+GPU performance can be integrated into small footprints.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description… TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy GenAI solutions on Google Cloud, unlocking new revenue streams and streamlining operations. Sreeni Alakappan says the work boosted developer productivity and compressed TTEC’s digital transformation timeline.
bullets: TTEC collaborated with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI plus Google Cloud infrastructure were used to streamline operations.
Efforts unlocked entirely new revenue streams for TTEC.
Developer productivity improved as a result of the deployments.
Consulting help compressed the company’s digital transformation timeline and expanded ambitions.
takeaways: Scalable cloud infrastructure is critical to accelerate GenAI deployments and realize new revenue opportunities.
Engaging cloud consulting can compress transformation timelines and de-risk rapid AI prototyping.
Improved developer productivity reduces time-to-market for AI-driven services, impacting capacity planning.
Operational efficiency gains from cloud-based GenAI can influence decisions on on-prem vs. cloud resource allocation.
